2026-04-17T08:08:42.0146061Z ##[group]Run # Generate a secure random API key (360 bits of entropy, 40+ chars)
2026-04-17T08:08:42.0146600Z [36;1m# Generate a secure random API key (360 bits of entropy, 40+ chars)[0m
2026-04-17T08:08:42.0147227Z [36;1m# Mask immediately to prevent timing vulnerabilities[0m
2026-04-17T08:08:42.0147595Z [36;1mAPI_KEY=$(openssl rand -base64 45 | tr -d '/+=')[0m
2026-04-17T08:08:42.0147911Z [36;1mecho "::add-mask::${API_KEY}"[0m
2026-04-17T08:08:42.0148157Z [36;1m[0m
2026-04-17T08:08:42.0148328Z [36;1mPORT=3001[0m
2026-04-17T08:08:42.0148519Z [36;1m[0m
2026-04-17T08:08:42.0148694Z [36;1m# Set outputs for next steps[0m
2026-04-17T08:08:42.0148934Z [36;1m{[0m
2026-04-17T08:08:42.0149142Z [36;1m  echo "safe_outputs_api_key=${API_KEY}"[0m
2026-04-17T08:08:42.0149430Z [36;1m  echo "safe_outputs_port=${PORT}"[0m
2026-04-17T08:08:42.0149699Z [36;1m} >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"[0m

CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 1 issue(s) can be created.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"body":{"description":"Detailed issue description in Markdown. Labels must exist in the repository.","items":{"type":"string"},"type":"array"},"parent":{"description":"Parent issue number for creating sub-issues. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 42 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/42). Can also be a temporary_id (e.g., 'aw_abc123', 'aw_Test123') from a previously created issue in the same workflow run.","type":["number","string"]},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"},"temporary_id":{"description":"Unique temporary identifier for referencing this issue before it's created. Format: 'aw_' followed by 3 to 12 alphanumeric characters (e.g., 'aw_abc1', 'aw_Test123'). Use '#aw_ID' in body text to reference other issues by their temporary_id; these are replaced with actual issue numbers after creation.","pattern":"^aw_[A-Za-z0-9]{3,12}$","type":"string"},"title":{"description":"Concise issue title summarizing the bug, feature, or task. The title appears as the main heading, so keep it brief and descriptive.","type":"string"}},"required":["title","body"],"type":"object"},"name":"create_issue"},{"description":"Report that a tool or capability needed to complete the task is not available, or share any information you deem important about missing functionality or limitations. Use this when you cannot accomplish what was requested because the required functionality is missing or access is restricted.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"alternatives":{"description":"Any workarounds, manual steps, or alternative approaches the user could take (max 256 characters).","type":"string"},"integrity":{"description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\").","type":"string"},"reason":{"description":"Explanation of why this tool is needed or what information you want to share about the limitation (max 256 characters).","type":"string"},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"},"tool":{"description":"Optional: Name or description of the missing tool or capability (max 128 characters). Be specific about what functionality is needed.","type":"string"}},"required":["reason"],"type":"object"},"name":"missing_tool"},{"description":"Log a transparency message when no significant actions are needed. Use this to confirm workflow completion and provide visibility when analysis is complete but no changes or outputs are required (e.g., 'No issues found', 'All checks passed'). This ensures the workflow produces human-visible output even when no other actions are taken.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"integrity":{"description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\").","type":"string"},"message":{"description":"Status or completion message to log. Should explain what was analyzed and the outcome (e.g., 'Code review complete - no issues found', 'Analysis complete - all tests passing').","type":"string"},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"}},"required":["message"],"type":"object"},"name":"noop"},{"description":"Report that data or information needed to complete the task is not available. Use this when you cannot accomplish what was requested because required data, context, or information is missing.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"alternatives":{"description":"Any workarounds, manual steps, or alternative approaches the user could take (max 256 characters).","type":"string"},"context":{"description":"Additional context about the missing data or where it should come from (max 256 characters).","type":"string"},"data_type":{"description":"Type or description of the missing data or information (max 128 characters). Be specific about what data is needed.","type":"string"},"integrity":{"description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\").","type":"string"},"reason":{"description":"Explanation of why this data is needed to complete the task (max 256 characters).","type":"string"},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"}},"required":[],"type":"object"},"name":"missing_data"},{"description":"Signal that the task could not be completed due to an infrastructure or tool failure (e.g., MCP server crash, missing authentication, inaccessible repository). Use this when required tools or data are unavailable and the task cannot be meaningfully performed. This is distinct from noop (no action needed) — it indicates an active failure that prevented the task from running. The workflow framework will treat this as a failure signal even when the agent exits successfully.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"details":{"description":"Optional extended details or diagnostic context about the failure (max 65000 characters).","type":"string"},"reason":{"description":"A concise explanation of why the task could not be completed (max 1024 characters). Be specific about which tools or resources were unavailable.","type":"string"}},"required":["reason"],"type":"object"},"name":"report_incomplete"},{"description":"Dispatch the issue triage workflow for the newly created issue","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{},"type":"object"},"name":"dispatch_triage"}]}}

2026-04-17T08:12:30.8670516Z {"timestamp":"2026-04-17T08:10:08.381849745Z","direction":"IN","type":"RESPONSE","server_id":"github","payload":{"id":1,"jsonrpc":"2.0","result":{"content":[{"text":"successfully downloaded text file (SHA: [REDACTED])","type":"text"},{"resource":{"mimeType":"text/plain; charset=utf-8","text":"# Release History\n\n## 1.0.0-beta.1 (Unreleased)\n\n### Features Added\n\n- Initial release of Azure.GeneratorAgent, an MCP (Model Context Protocol) server that exposes deterministic fix tools for automating Azure SDK code generation and migration workflows\n- MCP server mode (`McpServerHost.RunAsync`) serving tools over stdio transport with auto-discovery from the assembly at startup\n- `DeterministicFixRegistry` with 27 error-pattern-to-tool rules covering field renames, type pattern replacements, missing using directives, obsolete using removal, nullable annotations, and method call replacements\n- 19 individual MCP tool classes including regex replacements, nullable annotation fixes, code generation, build output parsing, error classification, test execution, commit iteration, project discovery, generated code snapshots, `[CodeGenSuppress]` attribute insertion, and finalization\n- `TestResult` structured return type for the `run_tests` tool with properties for `Success`, `ExitCode`, `Passed`, `Failed`, `Skipped`, `Total`, `Failures`, `Error`, and `RawOutput`\n- Skill-driven workflow via the dedicated `.github/skills/dpg-migration/SKILL.md` and `.github/skills/mpg-migration/SKILL.md` docs, where the LLM reads the appropriate skill, calls MCP tools directly, and reasons about what to do next\n- Cross-platform support with proper cancellation token handling\n","uri":"repo://Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/sha/[REDACTED]/contents/sdk/tools/Azure.GeneratorAgent/CHANGELOG.md"},"type":"resource"}]}}}
2026-04-17T08:12:30.8710623Z {"timestamp":"2026-04-17T08:10:08.393927047Z","direction":"IN","type":"RESPONSE","server_id":"github","payload":{"id":1,"jsonrpc":"2.0","result":{"content":[{"text":"successfully downloaded text file (SHA: [REDACTED])","type":"text"},{"resource":{"mimeType":"text/plain; charset=utf-8","text":"---\nname: mpg-migration\ndescription: Handles Azure SDK for .NET management-plane migrations from AutoRest/Swagger to TypeSpec; use for MPG, mgmt migration, or Azure.ResourceManager.* migration requests.\n---\n\n# MPG Migration Workflow\n\nMigrate an Azure SDK for .NET management library from AutoRest/Swagger to TypeSpec.\n\n## When to Use\n\nUse this skill only for **management-plane** packages named like `Azure.ResourceManager.*`.\n\nTypical triggers:\n- \"mpg migration\"\n- \"mgmt migration\"\n- \"migrate Azure.ResourceManager.\u003cService\u003e to TypeSpec\"\n\n## Required Input\n\n- SDK package path under `sdk/\u003cservice\u003e/Azure.ResourceManager.\u003cService\u003e/`\n- Local `azure-rest-api-specs` repo path if it is not present at `../azure-rest-api-specs`\n\n## Prerequisites\n\n- **SDK repo**: Package at `sdk/\u003cservice\u003e/Azure.ResourceManager.\u003cService\u003e/`\n- **Spec repo**: Sibling `../azure-rest-api-specs` with TypeSpec for this service. Ask user for path if not found.\n- **Branches**: `\u003cservice\u003e-mpg-migration` in both repos, branched off `main`\n\n## Guardrails\n\n1. **Never edit `src/Generated/` or `metadata.json` by hand.**\n2. **Never add `ApiCompatBaseline.txt` entries or disable ApiCompat/package validation.**\n3. **Prefer spec-side decorators first**: `@@clientName`, `@@access`, `@@alternateType`, `@@markAsPageable`.\n4. **Use MCP tools first for deterministic custom-code edits**; hand-edit only the remaining shim logic.\n5. **Use SDK customizations only** for backward-compat shims or when decorators cannot express the fix.\n\n## Default Autonomy\n\nProceed autonomously through the normal generate/build/fix loop. Ask the user only when:\n- the spec repo path is missing or ambiguous\n- the fix requires changes beyond `client.tsp`\n- the issue is a generator bug with no safe workaround\n\n## Phase 1 — Setup \u0026 First Generation\n\n1. **Check for `migration-status.md`** in the package directory. If it exists, read it and resume from the recorded phase — skip completed work.\n2. Sync both repos to latest `main`.\n3. Update `tsp-location.yaml`: set `emitterPackageJsonPath: eng/azure-typespec-http-client-csharp-mgmt-emitter-package.json`.\n4. Generate: `dotnet build /t:GenerateCode` in `src/`, or `RegenSdkLocal.ps1 -Services \"\u003cService\u003e\" -LocalSpecRepoPath \u003cpath\u003e`.\n5. Build — expect errors, proceed to Phase 2.\n\n## Phase 2 — Build-Fix Loop\n\nFix errors **spec-side** (decorators in `client.tsp` with `\"csharp\"` scope) or **SDK-side** (custom code in `src/Customization/`). Always verify zero swagger diff after spec changes.\n\nAfter each fix: regenerate if spec changed → rebuild → check remaining errors → repeat until clean.\nApply this same classify-first loop to both **compile/build errors** and **breaking-change remediation** from ApiCompat/API diff review.\n\nBefore fixing an issue, **categorize it first** so the mitigation goes to the right layer and we do not paper over the real cause.\n\n### Categorize build errors and breaking changes\n\n| Category | Typical symptoms | Preferred fix |\n|---------|-------------------|---------------|\n| **Spec-shape issue** | Wrong names, types, visibility, paging shape, inheritance, or operation grouping | Fix in `client.tsp` using decorators first |\n| **SDK compatibility gap** | ApiCompat failures such as missing types/members, signature changes, or lost convenience entry points | Add targeted compatibility shims in SDK custom code |\n| **Customization drift** | Existing custom code no longer compiles after regeneration because generated symbols moved or changed shape | Update or re-home the customization, then rebuild |\n| **Generated-code drift** | `Verify Generated Code` or local regen changes files after a clean build | Regenerate, export API, and commit the produced output |\n| **Generator bug** | Emitted code is structurally wrong and cannot be safely corrected with decorators or normal customizations | Stop, minimize the repro, and file a generator issue |\n\nDuring the loop, keep a running list of remaining issues grouped by category. This makes it clear which items are real migration breaks, which are compatibility shims, and which must be fixed in the generator.\n\nFor **SDK-side custom code**, prefer MCP tools for deterministic edits:\n- `add_using_directive` / `remove_using_directive`\n- `regex_replacement`\n- `nullable_annotation_fix`\n- `rename_codegen_type`\n- `add_codegen_suppress`\n\nUse them in a loop: **build/classify → batch MCP fixes → regenerate if `CodeGen*` attributes changed → hand-write only the remaining compatibility logic**. Use this same loop again in Phase 3 when mitigating breaking changes; do not treat ApiCompat work as a separate ad hoc cleanup step.\n\n### Spec-side decorator table\n\n| Problem | Decorator |\n|---------|-----------|\n| Wrong property type | `@@alternateType(Model.prop, targetType, \"csharp\")` |\n| Wrong name | `@@clientName(target, \"NewName\", \"csharp\")` |\n| Type should be public | `@@access(Model, Access.public, \"csharp\")` |\n| Model should be input and output | `@@usage(Model, Usage.input \\| Usage.output, \"csharp\")` |\n| Needs pageable return type | `@@markAsPageable(Interface.op, \"csharp\")` |\n| Flatten properties envelope | `@@flattenProperty(Model.properties, \"csharp\")` |\n| Change base type | `@@hierarchyBuilding(Model, TargetBase, \"csharp\")` |\n| Wrong operation parameter type | `@@alternateType(Interface.op::parameters.param, targetType, \"csharp\")` |\n| Operation name collision | `@@clientLocation(Interface.op, \"GroupName\", \"csharp\")` |\n\n### SDK-side customization (when decorators can't help)\n\nEvery file needs a justification comment.\n\n| Problem | Fix |\n|---------|-----|\n| Flattened properties lost (polymorphic type) | Shim properties delegating to `Properties` bag |\n| Protected constructor missing (discriminated base) | `protected` ctor in partial class |\n| Property lost due to `@@alternateType` model swap | Add property in partial class |\n\n## Phase 3 — Mitigate Breaking Changes\n\n1. Export API: `pwsh eng/scripts/Export-API.ps1 \u003cservice\u003e`\n2. Diff with `origin/main` API file.\n3. Categorize each break (shape issue vs compatibility gap vs generator bug), then fix it using the same **build/classify → fix → regenerate/export API → re-diff** loop from Phase 2 — **never** suppress with `ApiCompatBaseline.txt`.\n\n## Phase 4 — Self-Review\n\nReview changes against the `mpg-migration-pr-review` skill rules locally — check customization quality rules and decorator preference rules. Fix issues before proceeding.\n\nBefore opening the SDK PR:\n- Ensure `CHANGELOG.md` has a short migration note.\n- Ensure `ci.mgmt.yml` exists if the package does not already have one.\n\n## Phase 5 — Create PRs\n\n1. Run the `pre-commit-checks` skill on the SDK package.\n2. Push spec to fork → draft PR against `Azure/azure-rest-api-specs`.\n3. Update `tsp-location.yaml` to point to fork commit.\n4. Push SDK to fork → draft PR against `Azure/azure-sdk-for-net`.\n\nUse concise PR titles:\n- Spec PR: `Add csharp customizations for \u003cService\u003e migration`\n- SDK PR: `[Mgmt] \u003cPackageName\u003e: Migrate to TypeSpec`\n\n## Generator Bugs\n\n1. If the generated code is structurally wrong after removing stale customizations, stop and report it as a generator bug.\n2. Create an issue with a minimum TypeSpec repro.\n3. Document any approved workaround in `client.tsp` or SDK custom code and link the issue.\n4. If no safe workaround exists, pause migration until the fix is merged.\n\n## Pausing \u0026 Resuming\n\nWhen pausing, save `sdk/\u003cservice\u003e/Azure.ResourceManager.\u003cService\u003e/migration-status.md`:\n\n```markdown\n## Phase 2 — Fix Build Errors\nBlocked: [#58138](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues/58138) — CollectionResultOfT name collision\nRemaining breaks: ApplicationPatch base type change, model factory signatures\n```\n\nKeep it minimal — just current phase, blockers, and unfinished work. On resume, read this file first. Delete when migration merges.\n\n## Done When\n\n- `dotnet build` is clean\n- ApiCompat issues are mitigated without baselines\n- required review/validation steps are complete\n- `tsp-location.yaml` points to the final spec commit used by the PR\n","uri":"repo://Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/sha/[REDACTED]/contents/.github/skills/mpg-migration/SKILL.md"},"type":"resource"}]}}}
2026-04-17T08:12:30.8754661Z {"timestamp":"2026-04-17T08:10:08.420400955Z","direction":"IN","type":"RESPONSE","server_id":"github","payload":{"id":1,"jsonrpc":"2.0","result":{"content":[{"text":"successfully downloaded text file (SHA: [REDACTED])","type":"text"},{"resource":{"mimeType":"text/plain; charset=utf-8","text":"# Azure Generator Agent client library for .NET\n\nAn MCP (Model Context Protocol) server that exposes deterministic fix tools for automating Azure SDK code generation and migration workflows. A skill (LLM agent) calls these tools directly to handle rule-based fixes, keeping LLM usage for only non-deterministic reasoning.\n\n## Getting started\n\n### Prerequisites\n\n- [.NET 10.0](https://dotnet.microsoft.com/download/dotnet/10.0) or later\n- Git (for repository operations)\n\n### Build\n\nBuild from source within the azure-sdk-for-net repository:\n\n```bash\ncd sdk/tools/Azure.GeneratorAgent\ndotnet build\n```\n\n### Run the MCP server\n\nStart the MCP server over stdio transport:\n\n```bash\ndotnet run --project src/Azure.GeneratorAgent.csproj\n```\n\n#### Register in VS Code (GitHub Copilot)\n\nAdd the server to your VS Code `settings.json` under `mcp.servers`:\n\n```jsonc\n{\n  \"mcp\": {\n    \"servers\": {\n      \"azure-generator-agent\": {\n        \"command\": \"dotnet\",\n        \"args\": [\n          \"run\",\n          \"--project\",\n          \"\u003crepo-root\u003e/sdk/tools/Azure.GeneratorAgent/src/Azure.GeneratorAgent.csproj\"\n        ]\n      }\n    }\n  }\n}\n```\n\n#### Register in Claude Desktop\n\nAdd to your Claude Desktop `claude_desktop_config.json`:\n\n```jsonc\n{\n  \"mcpServers\": {\n    \"azure-generator-agent\": {\n      \"command\": \"dotnet\",\n      \"args\": [\n        \"run\",\n        \"--project\",\n        \"\u003crepo-root\u003e/sdk/tools/Azure.GeneratorAgent/src/Azure.GeneratorAgent.csproj\"\n      ]\n    }\n  }\n}\n```\n\nReplace `\u003crepo-root\u003e` with the absolute path to your local clone of `azure-sdk-for-net`.\n\n### Authenticate the client\n\nAzure.GeneratorAgent is a local development tool that operates on files in your local repository clone. It does not make any authenticated calls to Azure services, so no authentication or credentials are required.\n\n## Key concepts\n\nThe Azure Generator Agent automates SDK code generation workflows, including code generation from service specifications and migration to new SDK patterns.\n\n### Architecture\n\nThe agent uses a three-layer architecture:\n\n- **MCP Server** — Exposes deterministic fix tools over the [Model Context Protocol](https://modelcontextprotocol.io/) via stdio transport. Tools are auto-discovered from the assembly at startup.\n- **MCP Tools** — 19 individual tool classes covering project discovery, regex replacements (field renames, type patterns), adding/removing using directives, nullable annotation fixes, `[CodeGenSuppress]` attribute insertion, build output parsing, error classification, code generation, generated code snapshots, test execution, commit iteration, and finalization. Each tool supports both MCP (JSON) and in-process invocation.\n- **Skill-Driven Workflow** — The migration skill docs ([`dpg-migration`](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/blob/main/.github/skills/dpg-migration/SKILL.md) and [`mpg-migration`](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/blob/main/.github/skills/mpg-migration/SKILL.md)) are the orchestrators. The LLM reads the appropriate skill, calls MCP tools directly, and reasons about what to do next. No compiled C# orchestrator — the skill drives the build→classify→fix→rebuild loop.\n\n### MCP Tools\n\n| Tool | Description |\n|------|-------------|\n| `discover_project` | Discover project metadata: plane (dpg/mpg), package name, service directory, emitter config, custom code folder, API surface files, and tsp-location.yaml fields. Call this first to get all context needed for migration. |\n| `regex_replacement` | Perform regex-based find/replace in source files (field renames, type patterns, namespace fixes) |\n| `add_using_directive` | Add a using directive to a C# file if not already present |\n| `remove_using_directive` | Remove using directives matching a namespace pattern |\n| `nullable_annotation_fix` | Fix CS8625/CS8600 by adding `?` nullable annotation on a specific line |\n| `add_codegen_suppress` | Add a `[CodeGenSuppress]` attribute to a custom partial class to suppress a duplicate member from the generator. Scans `Generated/` to find the member signature. |\n| `batch_fix` | Apply multiple deterministic fixes in a single call |\n| `bu
